title: neonauclea purpurascens
intro: Neonauclea purpurascens, also known as the 'Purple Nauclea', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, particularly in Fiji, Samoa, and Tonga. It is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antimalarial properties, making it a potential remedy for fever, rheumatism, and malaria. In traditional medicine, Neonauclea purpurascens is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, or used as a poultice to apply its medicinal properties topically.
